evals.prompt: our signups on shadetreeit.biz look way down this week, can you dig into what happened?
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/check-setup.mjs first, gets ready:false (no config in the project), routes to references/setup.md and walks the user through setup (companion skills, gh/firebase auth, service account, config files). Does NOT fabricate analytics data or invent an analysis.

evals.prompt: run the daily website health check
evals.expected_output: Uses the pre-fetched raw JSON in .claude/wha-work/ (notes it as pre-provided rather than re-fetching), runs normalize-timeseries.mjs then detect-anomalies.mjs via the actual scripts, produces a report following references/daily-health-check.md's template, flags the injected signup drop as an anomaly with correct severity, does not invent a root cause without evidence.

evals.prompt: did one of our recent deploys cause the signup drop on 2026-07-07?
evals.expected_output: Uses the candidate list in .claude/wha-work/github-events.json; since the repo is fictional, gh diff inspection will fail — the agent must degrade honestly: state the candidate commit's temporal proximity and touched-files metadata, assign explicit Medium/Low confidence per references/code-remediation.md's rubric, and explicitly say diff inspection was not possible. It must NOT fabricate file/line-level fixes for code it never saw.
